summary: Joel briefly points readers to a discussion thread on his forum about offshoring and outsourcing in software development. Rather than writing his own take, he highlights a community discussion where a poster named Ken sets up an argument about offshoring that is then dismantled by international readers. Joel endorses the crowd-sourced debate as superior to anything he could have written himself.
key_points: Joel defers to his community's expertise on the offshoring/outsourcing debate rather than writing his own analysis
The forum discussion follows a classic debate structure: one person presents a position and others counter it
Joel implicitly acknowledges that some topics are better explored through diverse global perspectives than a single author's take
money_quotes: Ken sets up the strawman; eloquent readers from around the globe tear it down.
key_insight: Sometimes the best commentary a blogger can offer is pointing to a community discussion that captures more perspectives than any single author could.
